vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1257: Health classifier: classifyAgentKind misclassifies review/test/ship specialists as 'work', breaking workAgentCount cap

plan.title: Health classifier: classifyAgentKind misclassifies review/test/ship specialists as 'work', breaking workAgentCount cap
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-1249 fixed the runtime behavior of classifyAgentKind by adding an optional `role` parameter and routing agent-prefixed IDs through role-based discrimination. The reviewer reopened PAN-1257 on 2026-05-24 because the original acceptance criteria required (a) unit tests for the classifier covering each role value, (b) proof that health.summary.workAgentCount excludes specialists, and (c) regression coverage proving swarm dispatch succeeds when only other-issue specialists are running. None of those tests were added with PAN-1249. The implementation at src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts:169-187 is correct; the test coverage gap is the only remaining work.
narratives.Proposal: Two independently-mergeable beads. Bead 1 exports classifyAgentKind and adds a focused unit-test file covering every AC-cited agent ID (agent-pan-1228-review, agent-pan-1228-review-correctness, agent-pan-1228-ship, agent-pan-1190, agent-pan-1122-1), every role value (work, review, test, ship, undefined), and the legacy prefix branches (planning-*, specialist-*, *-agent). Bead 2 extends src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts with a regression test demonstrating the symptom is gone: with workAgentCount below cap (because specialists for other issues no longer inflate it), a swarm dispatch succeeds. Together these satisfy all eight AC checkboxes from the original issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Scope is test-only. Do not modify the classifier implementation.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1249 already shipped the runtime fix per the issue history and the live code at system-health-service.ts:169-187. The reopen comment from 2026-05-24 names test coverage as the sole remaining work. Touching the implementation would risk re-introducing the original bug.

autoDecisions.summary: Export classifyAgentKind for direct unit testing.
autoDecisions.rationale: The alternative (driving the classifier via getSystemHealthSnapshot) requires mocking /proc reads, tmux pane queries, docker stats, and the process table. That scaffolding cost is unjustified for a pure 18-line function. A named export is a one-keyword change with no runtime impact.

autoDecisions.summary: Split into two beads (classifier unit + swarm regression).
autoDecisions.rationale: Each bead is independently reviewable and independently mergeable. Bead 1 proves the function is correct in isolation; bead 2 proves the symptom-level AC ('swarm dispatch succeeds when only specialists are running for other issues'). Folding them together would make the diff harder to review and lose the symmetry between the two layers.

autoDecisions.summary: Use the parent Role value ('review'/'test'/'ship') in role= test args, not subRole suffixes.
autoDecisions.rationale: Role is defined in src/lib/agents.ts:58 as 'plan'|'work'|'review'|'test'|'ship'|'flywheel'|'strike'. Review convoys use a separate subRole field; the role field itself is always the parent. agent-pan-1228-review-correctness has role: 'review', not role: 'review-correctness'.

autoDecisions.summary: Place the unit test at src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/classify-agent-kind.test.ts (sibling of the existing __tests__ directory for system-health services).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the established colocation pattern (every other service test lives in this folder per `ls __tests__`). No new directories needed.

items.title: Export classifyAgentKind and add unit tests covering all role values and AC-cited agent IDs

narrative.Action: 1) In src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts, add the `export` keyword to the existing `function classifyAgentKind(...)` at line 169 (no other changes — preserve the function body, signature, and the PAN-1257 comment block). 2) Create src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/classify-agent-kind.test.ts that imports the now-exported classifier and asserts every case below. The file should use describe/it from vitest, no mocks needed (the function is pure), and follow the file-style of the other __tests__ entries. Each acceptance criterion below must correspond to at least one explicit test assertion using the literal agent ID from the AC.

subItems.title: Test asserts classifyAgentKind('agent-pan-1122-1', 'work') === 'work' (slot agent under modern naming)
Test asserts classifyAgentKind('agent-pan-1257', undefined) === 'work' (legacy state file with no role field still treated as work)
Test asserts legacy prefixes still classify correctly: 'planning-pan-1' -> 'planning', 'specialist-foo' -> 'specialist', 'merge-agent' -> 'specialist', 'unknown-id' -> 'other'

Test verifies summary count math from a mixed fixture: given 1 work + 5 review-variant + 1 ship + 1 test agents, agents.filter(a => a.kind === 'work').length === 1 and agents.filter(a => a.kind === 'specialist').length === 7
classifyAgentKind is exported as a named export from src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts (verifiable via the import in the new test file)
The new test file passes under `npm test` and adds zero new failures to the suite

items.title: Add swarm-dispatch regression test demonstrating capacity gate respects classifier fix

narrative.Action: Add a new `it(...)` block to src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts (alongside the existing `honors PAN_AGENT_BLOCK_COUNT=0 as an explicit hard stop` test at line 1772) that exercises the PAN-1257 symptom scenario: hardLimit=10, workAgentCount=1 (because the 7 specialists running for other issues correctly classify as 'specialist' and don't inflate workAgentCount). Use vi.mocked(systemHealthService.getSystemHealthSnapshot).mockResolvedValueOnce({ summary: { workAgentCount: 1 } } as any) to override the global beforeEach stub. Assert that dispatchSwarmWave returns status 200 (or whatever the existing non-blocked path returns) and NOT 429 with 'No agent capacity available'. Comment the test header with 'Regression: PAN-1257 — specialists for other issues must not consume work-agent capacity slots.'

subItems.title: Test in swarm.test.ts asserts swarm dispatch returns status !== 429 when workAgentCount=1 and hardLimit=10
Test body includes a comment referencing PAN-1257 so future grep-able regression context is preserved
Test does not regress the existing 'honors PAN_AGENT_BLOCK_COUNT=0' test — both still pass under `npm test -- swarm.test.ts`
Test cleans up any env var or mock-resolved-value-once side effects (no global state leakage into sibling tests)
